# fhir-test-data

> Version 0.1.3. A TypeScript CLI and library for generating valid FHIR R4 / R4B / R5
> test resources with country-aware identifiers — across 14 locales, with seeded determinism.

`fhir-test-data` generates structurally valid FHIR resources for testing. Identifiers pass
each country's official check-digit algorithm. Clinical resources use real LOINC and SNOMED CT
codes with values in realistic clinical ranges. All output is deterministic: the same seed
produces the same data on any machine.

The browser-safe TypeScript core works in Node.js, Deno, and browser bundles. The CLI
writes to stdout by default, making it pipe-friendly.

## When to use this library

- You need realistic FHIR test data for unit tests, integration tests, or CI pipelines
- You are building a FHIR server and need fixtures to test with
- You need identifiers from a specific country (NHS numbers, BSNs, RRNs, CPFs, etc.)
- You want deterministic fixtures you can commit and reproduce

## Installation

```bash
npm install fhir-test-data       # library
npm install -g fhir-test-data    # global CLI
```

## Key CLI commands

```bash
# Generate a patient with a UK NHS number
fhir-test-data generate patient --locale uk --seed 42

# Generate 10 patients as NDJSON
fhir-test-data generate patient --count 10 --format ndjson

# Generate a full bundle with all resource types
fhir-test-data generate bundle --locale us --seed 1

# Save to a fixtures directory
fhir-test-data generate bundle --locale au --seed 1 --output ./fixtures/

# Generate with a specific FHIR version
fhir-test-data generate patient --locale de --fhir-version R5

# Generate an encounter resource
fhir-test-data generate encounter --locale us --seed 42

# Generate a diagnostic report
fhir-test-data generate diagnostic-report --locale uk --seed 10

# Inject a fault to test validation pipelines
fhir-test-data generate patient --locale us --seed 1 --faults missing-resource-type

# Pipe into a validator
fhir-test-data generate patient --locale uk --seed 42 | \
  fhir-resource-diff validate - --fhir-version R4
```

## Supported resource types

`Patient`, `Practitioner`, `PractitionerRole`, `Organization`, `Observation`, `Condition`,
`AllergyIntolerance`, `MedicationStatement`, `Encounter`, `DiagnosticReport`, `Bundle`

## Supported locales

14 locales with check-digit-validated national identifiers:

| Code | Country | Patient identifiers |
|---|---|---|
| `us` | United States | SSN, MRN |
| `uk` | United Kingdom | NHS Number (Modulus 11) |
| `au` | Australia | IHI, Medicare (Luhn) |
| `ca` | Canada | Ontario HCN |
| `de` | Germany | KVID-10 |
| `fr` | France | NIR (Modulus 97) |
| `nl` | Netherlands | BSN (11-proef) |
| `in` | India | Aadhaar (Verhoeff), ABHA |
| `jp` | Japan | Hospital MRN |
| `kr` | South Korea | RRN (with gender encoding) |
| `sg` | Singapore | NRIC / FIN (check letter) |
| `br` | Brazil | CPF (Modulus 11 variant) |
| `mx` | Mexico | CURP |
| `za` | South Africa | SA ID Number (Luhn) |

## TypeScript library API

```typescript
import {
  createPatientBuilder,
  createEncounterBuilder,
  createDiagnosticReportBuilder,
  createBundleBuilder,
} from "fhir-test-data";

// Generate a single UK patient
const [patient] = createPatientBuilder().locale("uk").seed(42).build();

// Generate an encounter linked to a patient
const [encounter] = createEncounterBuilder()
  .locale("us")
  .seed(1)
  .subject("Patient/abc-123")
  .fhirVersion("R4")
  .build();

// Generate a diagnostic report
const [report] = createDiagnosticReportBuilder()
  .seed(5)
  .subject("Patient/abc-123")
  .build();

// Generate a full bundle
const [bundle] = createBundleBuilder()
  .locale("us")
  .seed(42)
  .type("transaction")
  .clinicalResourcesPerPatient(5)
  .build();
```

## Fault injection

Generate intentionally invalid resources for testing validation pipelines:

```typescript
import { withFault } from "fhir-test-data/faults";

const [invalid] = withFault(
  createPatientBuilder().locale("uk").seed(42),
  "missing-resource-type"
).build();
```

Available fault types: `missing-resource-type`, `invalid-resource-type`, `missing-id`,
`invalid-gender`, `malformed-date`, `empty-name`, `wrong-type-on-field`,
`duplicate-identifier`, `invalid-telecom-system`, `missing-status`, `invalid-status-value`, `random`

## Seeded determinism

The same seed produces identical output on any machine:

```bash
fhir-test-data generate patient --locale uk --seed 42 > a.json
fhir-test-data generate patient --locale uk --seed 42 > b.json
diff a.json b.json  # empty — identical output
```

## Companion tools

- [`fhir-resource-diff`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/fhir-resource-diff) — validate and diff the resources you generate
- [`fhir-capability-analyzer`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/fhir-capability-analyzer) — check what a target FHIR server actually supports before running tests

- [Documentation](https://dnlbox.github.io/fhir-test-data/)
- [npm](https://www.npmjs.com/package/fhir-test-data)
- [GitHub](https://github.com/dnlbox/fhir-test-data)
- [FHIR specification](https://hl7.org/fhir/)
